articleTitle: The stress reduction potential of Bhagavad Gita and Yoga for healthcare workers during the COVID-19 pandemic: A randomized controlled trial.
takeaways: Healthcare workers practicing yoga during the COVID-19 pandemic showed measurable reductions in psychological distress compared to control groups
Bhagavad Gita learning sessions provided stress reduction benefits for healthcare workers, offering a culturally-embedded alternative to traditional mental health interventions
The combination of yoga and Bhagavad Gita study (Yoga+Gita) was tested as an integrated approach for managing work-related psychological stress in healthcare settings
Culturally familiar stress reduction methods like yoga and philosophical study demonstrated effectiveness in reducing pandemic-related psychological distress among healthcare workers
